name: Avenc Miner de s'Aliga
starting_point: Na Burguesa viewpoint (Génova). Walk to Coll des Pastors crossroads, take the left path then right, descending to a small stream. Follow the ascending trail to the Marble Caves, then descend to Coma de s'Aliga and take the right path toward the old mines. The chasm is about 20 m above the second mine entrance
coordinates_utm: DATUM EUROPEAN 1950: 461570 E, 4380210 N. DATUM WGS84: 461468 E, 4380010 N
equipment: Vertical progression equipment, one 40 m rope, plus 4 protectors
description: A natural cave combined with a historic mining complex, with total dimensions of 30 x 40 x 20 m. Unlike the neighboring Cova Minera de sa Punta which was stripped of its ornamentation, this cave retains some original formations including a beautiful column and stalagmites. The mine features two artificial galleries for mineral extraction: an upper gallery at -26 m and a lower gallery extending about 120 m. Access involves a steep ramp with a 10 m drop and significant rockfall danger during rope operations.
full_description:
Located near the Cova Minera de sa Punta and the Cova Minera devora sa Punta, its total dimensions are 30 x 40 x 20 m. Unlike its neighbor Cova Minera de sa Punta, which was stripped of all its ornamentation, in this case the emptying of the cavity left zones untouched that can give us an idea of what it once was. This mining complex consists of a natural cavity and two artificial galleries (Upper Gallery and Lower Gallery) for the extraction of materials.

To access the mining work area, one must descend a steeply inclined ramp that ends in an approximately ten-meter drop. During the descent along the ramp, it is easy for rock falls to occur at any moment due to the movement of the rope. The danger is real, so maximum attention must be paid during entry and exit maneuvers to the cavity.

Although initially the mineral extraction was possibly done through the natural mouth of the chasm, later, as they went deeper, it became necessary to build a first extraction gallery at -26 m (Upper Gallery) and another at -46 m (Lower Gallery), reaching the cut or face. The work was not completed in the chimney connection area.

Natural Cavity

The natural cavity begins in the so-called Saleta de s'Entrada (Entrance Little Hall), which is at a depth of -26 m, and through which we will move until surpassing some blocks and reaching a first step in the Main Hall. To our right, it is still possible to see the access to the Upper Gallery. A ramp allows us to ascend to a flowstone where some concretioning is visible, with a beautiful column and a group of stalagmites standing out, some of them decapitated.

Returning to the initial step mentioned above, we can descend to a lower level in search of the end. To our left is a large volume of carefully stacked rocks, and next to them, the beginning of a small room where the work was concluded and where the connection with the Lower Gallery should have been made.

Returning toward the Saleta de s'Entrada, one can climb to our right to a beautifully concretioned corner.

Upper Gallery

With a length of approximately 45 m, the Upper Gallery is blocked a few meters from the masonry portal that gives access to it. The rest of the gallery can be accessed from the Main Hall.

Lower Gallery

The Lower Gallery is 20 m below the Upper Gallery and its length is approximately 120 m.

Neither the ceiling of the gallery nor the side walls have needed to be shored up or lined, except at very specific points where pine wood props and masonry work have been used. On our last visit, the Lower Gallery was in good condition, and gypsum outcrops could be found.
